name: MOF-5
description: MOF-5 (also IRMOF-1) is the landmark Zn-based MOF that launched the modern era of reticular chemistry. It was the first MOF shown to have a permanent, ultrahigh porosity accessible for gas storage. The cubic structure features Zn4O clusters bridged by BDC linkers in a primitive cubic net. It remains the benchmark for evaluating new porous materials.

references.authors: Li, H.; Eddaoudi, M.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Design and synthesis of an exceptionally stable and highly porous metal-organic framework

references.authors: Rowsell, J. L. C.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Metal–organic frameworks: a new class of porous materials

references.authors: Furukawa, H. et al.
references.title: Ultrahigh Porosity in Metal-Organic Frameworks

name: HKUST-1
group: Williams (Hong Kong Univ. of Science & Technology)
description: HKUST-1 features the iconic Cu2 paddlewheel SBU bridged by tritopic BTC linkers to form a tbo topology with three distinct cage environments. The open Cu² coordination sites after solvent removal act as Lewis acid sites. It is the most studied MOF commercially and is sold by Sigma-Aldrich as Basolite C300.

references.authors: Chui, S. S.-Y.; Lo, S. M.-F.; Charmant, J. P. H.; Orpen, A. G.; Williams, I. D.
references.title: A Chemically Functionalizable Nanoporous Material [Cu3(TMA)2(H2O)3]n

references.authors: Rowsell, J. L. C.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Metal–organic frameworks: a new class of porous materials

name: ZIF-8
description: ZIF-8 adopts the sodalite (sod) topology with Zn²⁺ bridged by 2-methylimidazolate in a framework that is isotypic with zeolite sodalite. The narrow pore aperture (3.4 Å) gives excellent molecular sieving properties yet the flexible framework shows a 'gate-opening' phenomenon. Exceptionally thermally (up to 550°C in N₂) and chemically stable.

references.authors: Park, K. S. et al.
references.title: Exceptional chemical and thermal stability of zeolitic imidazolate frameworks

references.authors: Banerjee, R. et al.
references.title: High-Throughput Synthesis of Zeolitic Imidazolate Frameworks and Application to CO2 Capture

references.authors: Phan, A. et al.
references.title: Synthesis, structure, and carbon dioxide capture properties of zeolitic imidazolate frameworks

name: UiO-66
description: UiO-66 (Universitetet i Oslo-66) is the most studied zirconium MOF. The Zr6O4(OH)4 oxo-cluster SBU is 12-connected by BDC linkers in an fcu topology, giving exceptional thermal stability (up to ~500°C) and water/chemical resistance. The linker-missing defect chemistry in UiO-66 provides Lewis and Brønsted acid sites. It is available commercially.

references.authors: Cavka, J. H. et al.
references.title: A New Zirconium Inorganic Building Brick Forming Metal Organic Frameworks with Exceptional Stability

references.authors: Valenzano, L. et al.
references.title: Disclosing the Complex Structure of UiO-66 Metal Organic Framework: A Synergic Combination of Experiment and Theory

name: MIL-53
description: MIL-53(Al) is the archetypal 'breathing' MOF. Under guest adsorption/desorption, the structure reversibly transforms between a large-pore (lp) and narrow-pore (np) form with a ~40% unit cell volume change. This structural flexibility is driven by host–guest and host–host interactions. MIL-53 variants exist for Cr, Fe, V, Ga, Sc and In.

references.authors: Serre, C. et al.
references.title: Role of Solvent-Host Interactions That Lead to Very Large Swelling of Hybrid Frameworks

references.authors: Loiseau, T. et al.
references.title: A Rationale for the Large Breathing of the Porous Aluminum Terephthalate (MIL-53) upon Hydration

name: MIL-101(Cr)
description: MIL-101(Cr) holds one of the largest unit cells (a ≈ 88.9 Å) among MOFs, with a giant MTN zeotype topology containing two distinct cage types (25 and 29 Å). Its record-breaking surface area (up to 5900 m²/g) and large pores make it exceptional for bulky molecule loading. Chromium(III) trimeric clusters are bridged by BDC in a supertetrahedron-based architecture.

references.authors: Férey, G. et al.
references.title: A Chromium Terephthalate-Based Solid with Unusually Large Pore Volumes and Surface Area

references.authors: Hong, D.-Y. et al.
references.title: Selective CO2 Adsorption of an Amine-Functionalized Mixed-Ligand Metal-Organic Framework

name: NOTT-112
description: NOTT-112 features a large isophthalate-based hexatopic linker creating an exceptionally high-porosity tbo topology framework. The open Cu² sites and large pore volume give it among the highest gravimetric H₂ uptakes reported. It is a benchmark material for high-pressure gas storage evaluation.

references.authors: Yan, Y. et al.
references.title: Exceptionally High H2 Storage by a Metal-Organic Polyhedral Framework

name: MOF-74
description: MOF-74 (CPO-27) features one-dimensional hexagonal channels with a high density of open metal coordination sites on the channel walls. The DOBDC linker provides two hydroxyl groups that bind directly to the metal. The M-MOF-74 isoreticular series spans Mg, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn — with Mg-MOF-74 showing outstanding CO₂ uptake due to strong electrostatic binding.

references.authors: Rosi, N. L. et al.
references.title: Infinite Secondary Building Units and Forbidden Catenation in Metal-Organic Frameworks

references.authors: Dietzel, P. D. C. et al.
references.title: Adsorption properties and structure of CO2 adsorbed on open coordination sites of metal–organic framework Ni2(dhtp)

name: MIL-100(Fe)
description: MIL-100(Fe) is an Fe³⁺ analogue of MIL-101 based on BTC (trimesic acid) rather than BDC. Its MTN zeotype cage topology contains two types of mesoporous cages. Iron(III) trimeric clusters confer redox activity (Fe³⁺/Fe²⁺ switching). The material is considered biocompatible, making it attractive for drug delivery and MRI contrast applications.

references.authors: Horcajada, P. et al.
references.title: Flexible porous metal-organic frameworks for a controlled drug delivery

references.authors: Horcajada, P. et al.
references.title: Porous metal–organic-framework nanoscale carriers as a potential platform for drug delivery and imaging

name: PCN-14
description: PCN-14 contains anthracene-based octacarboxylate linkers forming an nbo topology with Cu paddlewheel SBUs. At the time of its report it held the record for volumetric methane storage (230 v/v at 290 K, 35 bar), exceeding the DOE target. The hydrophobic anthracene core provides enhanced methane uptake via dispersion interactions.

references.authors: Ma, S. et al.
references.title: Metal-Organic Framework from an Anthracene Derivative Containing Nanoscopic Cages Exhibiting High Methane Uptake

name: NU-125
description: NU-125 uses a triazine-based hexatopic isophthalate linker designed to maximise packing efficiency of Cu paddlewheel nodes. The tbo topology network gives excellent volumetric methane storage. The triazine core adds Lewis-basic nitrogen sites that enhance CO₂ binding.

references.authors: Wilmer, C. E. et al.
references.title: Large-scale screening of hypothetical metal–organic frameworks

references.authors: Farha, O. K. et al.
references.title: Metal–organic framework materials with ultrahigh surface areas

name: MOF-177
description: MOF-177 extends the IRMOF series to a large tritopic BTB linker generating the qom topology. At the time of its report it had the highest BET surface area of any known porous material (4500 m²/g). The giant cages accommodate large organic molecules. It exemplifies the design principle of expanding surface area via longer linkers in the Zn4O SBU series.

references.authors: Chae, H. K. et al.
references.title: A route to high surface area, porosity and inclusion of large molecules in crystals

name: NU-1000
description: NU-1000 is a mesoporous Zr-based MOF with large hexagonal and triangular pores suitable for enzyme and nanoparticle immobilisation. The 8-connected Zr6 node in csq topology leaves four aqua/hydroxyl sites per node for post-synthetic metalation. It has become the benchmark platform for Zr-MOF surface chemistry and ALD infiltration.

references.authors: Mondloch, J. E. et al.
references.title: Vapor-Phase Metalation by Atomic Layer Deposition in a Metal–Organic Framework

references.authors: Klet, R. C. et al.
references.title: Evaluation of Brønsted acid and base sites in Zr- and Hf-based metal–organic frameworks using potentiometric acid–base titration

name: ZIF-67
description: ZIF-67 is the Co²⁺ analogue of ZIF-8, isostructural and isoreticular in the sod topology. Its purple colour results from d-d transitions of Co²⁺. It has attracted enormous interest as a sacrificial template for derived cobalt oxide electrocatalysts (hollow Co₃O₄ polyhedra for OER) and as a CO₂ separation membrane material.

references.authors: Banerjee, R. et al.
references.title: High-Throughput Synthesis of Zeolitic Imidazolate Frameworks and Application to CO2 Capture

references.authors: Hu, J. et al.
references.title: ZIF-67-Derived Co3O4 Hollow Polyhedra as High-Performance Anodes for Lithium-Ion Batteries

name: SIFSIX-3-Ni
sbu: Ni(pyz)4 square planar node + SiF6²⁻ pillars
description: SIFSIX-3-Ni features a pillared-square-net topology where SiF6²⁻ pillar anions between Ni(pyrazine)4 layers create uniform pore channels (3.84 Å). The fluorine atoms line the pores and provide strong electrostatic CO₂ binding. It captures CO₂ from flue gas and even directly from air at 400 ppm concentration with unprecedented selectivity over N₂.

references.authors: Shekhah, O. et al.
references.title: Made-to-order metal-organic frameworks for trace carbon dioxide removal and air capture

references.authors: Cadiau, A. et al.
references.title: Designed CO2-selective metal-organic framework-based porous polymer networks

name: MIL-125
description: MIL-125 is the first Ti-based MOF with demonstrated photoredox activity. The Ti8O8(OH)4 oxo-cluster SBU is unique — eight Ti centres form a ring bridged by μ2-oxo and μ2-hydroxo groups. Under UV irradiation, Ti⁴⁺ is reduced to Ti³⁺, enabling photocatalytic alcohol oxidation and H₂ evolution. NH2-MIL-125 extends activity into visible light.

references.authors: Dan-Hardi, M. et al.
references.title: A New Photoactive Crystalline Highly Porous Titanium(IV) Dicarboxylate

references.authors: Fu, Y. et al.
references.title: An Amine-Functionalized Titanium Metal-Organic Framework Photocatalyst with Visible-Light-Induced Activity for CO2 Reduction

name: IRMOF-3
description: IRMOF-3 is the amine-functionalised analogue of MOF-5 (IRMOF-1), demonstrating the isoreticular approach to functional pore engineering. The free amino groups on the BDC linker provide reactive handles for post-synthetic covalent modification. This established PSM as a central strategy for MOF chemistry — converting IRMOF-3 into a library of functional derivatives via simple condensation reactions.

references.authors: Eddaoudi, M. et al.
references.title: Systematic Design of Pore Size and Functionality in Isoreticular MOFs and Their Application in Methane Storage

references.authors: Wang, Z.; Cohen, S. M.
references.title: Postsynthetic modification of metal–organic frameworks

name: BUT-12
description: BUT-12 combines Cr3O trimeric clusters with azobenzenetetracarboxylate linkers in an soc topology. The azobenzene moiety undergoes trans-to-cis photoisomerisation under UV light, enabling light-controlled modulation of gas sorption capacity. This makes BUT-12 a photoswitchable MOF with reversible on/off CO₂ sorption control.

references.authors: Luo, X.-Z. et al.
references.title: A microporous hydrogen-bonded organic framework: exceptional stability and highly selective adsorption of water over methanol

name: ZIF-90
description: ZIF-90 is the aldehyde-functionalised analogue of ZIF-8 in the sodalite topology. The aldehyde groups on the imidazolate ring project into the pore space and can be used for covalent post-synthetic modification (e.g. imine condensation with amino compounds). ZIF-90 was one of the first ZIFs used to fabricate mixed-matrix membranes with commercial polymers for CO₂/CH₄ separation.

references.authors: Morris, W. et al.
references.title: A Combined Experimental–Computational Study on the Effect of Topology on Carbon Dioxide Adsorption in Zeolitic Imidazolate Frameworks

references.authors: Venna, S. R.; Carreon, M. A.
references.title: Highly Permeable Zeolite Imidazolate Framework-8 Membranes for CO2/CH4 Separation

name: Mg-MOF-74
description: Mg-MOF-74 holds the highest CO₂ uptake per gram under relevant conditions (1 bar, 298 K) of any MOF — 8.04 mmol/g, exceeding zeolite 13X. The Mg²⁺ open coordination sites provide the strongest CO₂ binding enthalpy in the M-MOF-74 series (−47 kJ/mol). Pilot-scale flue gas separation tests have been conducted, and it is considered the benchmark for physisorptive CO₂ capture.

references.authors: Queen, W. L. et al.
references.title: Comprehensive study of carbon dioxide adsorption in the metal–organic frameworks M2(dobdc)

references.authors: Mason, J. A. et al.
references.title: Evaluating metal–organic frameworks for post-combustion carbon dioxide capture via temperature swing adsorption

name: IRMOF-8
description: IRMOF-8 extends the IRMOF series by replacing BDC with the longer 2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylate linker. The expanded cubic Zn4O–dicarboxylate net offers larger pores than MOF-5 while retaining the same pcu topology, demonstrating isoreticular synthesis.

references.authors: Eddaoudi, M.; Kim, J.; Rosi, N.; Vodak, D.; Wachter, J.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Systematic Design of Pore Size and Functionality in Isoreticular MOFs and Their Application in Methane Storage

name: IRMOF-10
description: IRMOF-10 employs the biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate linker to give an isoreticular pcu net with substantially larger pores than IRMOF-1/MOF-5. Non-interpenetrated polymorph; its interpenetrated cousin is IRMOF-9.

references.authors: Eddaoudi, M.; Kim, J.; Rosi, N.; Vodak, D.; Wachter, J.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Systematic Design of Pore Size and Functionality in Isoreticular MOFs and Their Application in Methane Storage

name: IRMOF-9
description: IRMOF-9 is the doubly interpenetrated polymorph of IRMOF-10. Interpenetration reduces the void volume but yields a more rigid structure with smaller, well-defined channels.

references.authors: Eddaoudi, M.; Kim, J.; Rosi, N.; Vodak, D.; Wachter, J.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Systematic Design of Pore Size and Functionality in Isoreticular MOFs and Their Application in Methane Storage

name: IRMOF-16
description: IRMOF-16 is the most expanded member of the original IRMOF series, with TPDC stretching the Zn4O cubic net to the limit of stability. Theoretical free volume exceeds 90 %, but the as-synthesised material is difficult to activate without collapse.

references.authors: Eddaoudi, M.; Kim, J.; Rosi, N.; Vodak, D.; Wachter, J.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Systematic Design of Pore Size and Functionality in Isoreticular MOFs and Their Application in Methane Storage

name: MOF-200
description: MOF-200 pairs Zn4O clusters with the giant tritopic BBC linker to produce a non-interpenetrated qom net with one of the largest pore volumes ever measured for a MOF. The Langmuir surface area reaches 10,400 m²/g.

references.authors: Furukawa, H.; Ko, N.; Go, Y. B.; Aratani, N.; Choi, S. B.; Choi, E.; Yazaydin, A. Ö.; Snurr, R. Q.; O'Keeffe, M.; Kim, J.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Ultrahigh Porosity in Metal–Organic Frameworks

name: MOF-205
description: MOF-205 is a mixed-ligand Zn4O framework combining a tritopic (BTB) and a ditopic (NDC) linker to give a complex multicomponent net with high surface area and well-defined two-pore-size architecture.

references.authors: Furukawa, H.; Ko, N.; Go, Y. B.; Aratani, N.; Choi, S. B.; Choi, E.; Yazaydin, A. Ö.; Snurr, R. Q.; O'Keeffe, M.; Kim, J.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Ultrahigh Porosity in Metal–Organic Frameworks

name: MOF-210
description: MOF-210 was for years the benchmark for ultrahigh-porosity MOFs. Its BET surface area of 6,240 m²/g and Langmuir area of 10,400 m²/g remain among the highest values reported, achieved through mixed BTE/BPDC linkers in a non-interpenetrated toz net.

references.authors: Furukawa, H.; Ko, N.; Go, Y. B.; Aratani, N.; Choi, S. B.; Choi, E.; Yazaydin, A. Ö.; Snurr, R. Q.; O'Keeffe, M.; Kim, J.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Ultrahigh Porosity in Metal–Organic Frameworks

name: MOF-801
description: MOF-801 is the Zr6–fumarate analogue of UiO-66 with the smallest ditopic carboxylate linker. Its sharp step-shaped water isotherm in the 20–40% RH window underpins atmospheric water harvesting devices that operate in arid climates.

references.authors: Furukawa, H.; Gándara, F.; Zhang, Y.-B.; Jiang, J.; Queen, W. L.; Hudson, M. R.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Water Adsorption in Porous Metal–Organic Frameworks and Related Materials

references.authors: Kim, H.; Yang, S.; Rao, S. R.; Narayanan, S.; Kapustin, E. A.; Furukawa, H.; Umans, A. S.; Yaghi, O. M.; Wang, E. N.
references.title: Water harvesting from air with metal-organic frameworks powered by natural sunlight

name: MOF-808
description: MOF-808 reduces the Zr6 cluster connectivity from 12 (UiO-66) to 6 by using the tritopic BTC linker, exposing six open Zr sites per cluster after activation. The defect-rich Zr6 nodes make it one of the most active heterogeneous Lewis acid catalysts in the MOF family.

references.authors: Furukawa, H.; Gándara, F.; Zhang, Y.-B.; Jiang, J.; Queen, W. L.; Hudson, M. R.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Water Adsorption in Porous Metal–Organic Frameworks and Related Materials

references.authors: Moon, S.-Y.; Liu, Y.; Hupp, J. T.; Farha, O. K.
references.title: Instantaneous Hydrolysis of Nerve-Agent Simulants with a Six-Connected Zirconium-Based Metal-Organic Framework

name: MOF-303
description: MOF-303 outperforms MOF-801 in atmospheric water harvesting, with a steeper sub-10% RH isotherm and excellent cycling stability. Its Al-pyrazoledicarboxylate framework is the basis of solar-driven harvester prototypes demonstrated in the Mojave Desert.

references.authors: Hanikel, N.; Prévot, M. S.; Fathieh, F.; Kapustin, E. A.; Lyu, H.; Wang, H.; Diercks, N. J.; Glover, T. G.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Rapid Cycling and Exceptional Yield in a Metal-Organic Framework Water Harvester

name: MOF-525
description: MOF-525 was the first Zr6-porphyrin framework, joining the porous robustness of Zr6 clusters with the redox and photophysical activity of porphyrins. Metal insertion in the porphyrin pocket yields catalytically active variants (Fe, Co, Mn, Cu).

references.authors: Morris, W.; Volosskiy, B.; Demir, S.; Gándara, F.; McGrier, P. L.; Furukawa, H.; Cascio, D.; Stoddart, J. F.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Synthesis, Structure, and Metalation of Two New Highly Porous Zirconium Metal-Organic Frameworks

name: Ni-MOF-74
description: Ni-MOF-74 (also known as CPO-27-Ni) belongs to the M-MOF-74 family in which 1D metal-oxide rods running along the c-axis decorate a hexagonal honeycomb (etb) of one-dimensional channels. Removal of axial solvent uncovers coordinatively unsaturated Ni(II) sites that bind CO₂, alkenes, and small molecules with characteristic enthalpies.

references.authors: Caskey, S. R.; Wong-Foy, A. G.; Matzger, A. J.
references.title: Dramatic Tuning of Carbon Dioxide Uptake via Metal Substitution in a Coordination Polymer with Cylindrical Pores

references.authors: Dietzel, P. D. C.; Morita, Y.; Blom, R.; Fjellvåg, H.
references.title: An In Situ High-Temperature Single-Crystal Investigation of a Dehydrated MOF Compound

name: Co-MOF-74
description: Co-MOF-74 (also known as CPO-27-Co) belongs to the M-MOF-74 family in which 1D metal-oxide rods running along the c-axis decorate a hexagonal honeycomb (etb) of one-dimensional channels. Removal of axial solvent uncovers coordinatively unsaturated Co(II) sites that bind CO₂, alkenes, and small molecules with characteristic enthalpies.

references.authors: Caskey, S. R.; Wong-Foy, A. G.; Matzger, A. J.
references.title: Dramatic Tuning of Carbon Dioxide Uptake via Metal Substitution in a Coordination Polymer with Cylindrical Pores

references.authors: Dietzel, P. D. C.; Morita, Y.; Blom, R.; Fjellvåg, H.
references.title: An In Situ High-Temperature Single-Crystal Investigation of a Dehydrated MOF Compound

name: Zn-MOF-74
description: Zn-MOF-74 (also known as CPO-27-Zn) belongs to the M-MOF-74 family in which 1D metal-oxide rods running along the c-axis decorate a hexagonal honeycomb (etb) of one-dimensional channels. Removal of axial solvent uncovers coordinatively unsaturated Zn(II) sites that bind CO₂, alkenes, and small molecules with characteristic enthalpies.

references.authors: Caskey, S. R.; Wong-Foy, A. G.; Matzger, A. J.
references.title: Dramatic Tuning of Carbon Dioxide Uptake via Metal Substitution in a Coordination Polymer with Cylindrical Pores

references.authors: Dietzel, P. D. C.; Morita, Y.; Blom, R.; Fjellvåg, H.
references.title: An In Situ High-Temperature Single-Crystal Investigation of a Dehydrated MOF Compound

name: Fe-MOF-74
description: Fe-MOF-74 (also known as CPO-27-Fe) belongs to the M-MOF-74 family in which 1D metal-oxide rods running along the c-axis decorate a hexagonal honeycomb (etb) of one-dimensional channels. Removal of axial solvent uncovers coordinatively unsaturated Fe(II) sites that bind CO₂, alkenes, and small molecules with characteristic enthalpies.

references.authors: Caskey, S. R.; Wong-Foy, A. G.; Matzger, A. J.
references.title: Dramatic Tuning of Carbon Dioxide Uptake via Metal Substitution in a Coordination Polymer with Cylindrical Pores

references.authors: Dietzel, P. D. C.; Morita, Y.; Blom, R.; Fjellvåg, H.
references.title: An In Situ High-Temperature Single-Crystal Investigation of a Dehydrated MOF Compound

name: UiO-66-NH₂
description: The amino-functionalised analogue of UiO-66 retains the parent fcu topology while adding free –NH₂ groups along the pore walls. These boost CO₂ affinity, enable post-synthetic modification, and red-shift the absorption to make the framework visible-light photocatalytic.

references.authors: Kandiah, M.; Nilsen, M. H.; Usseglio, S.; Jakobsen, S.; Olsbye, U.; Tilset, M.; Larabi, C.; Quadrelli, E. A.; Bonino, F.; Lillerud, K. P.
references.title: Synthesis and Stability of Tagged UiO-66 Zr-MOFs

name: UiO-67
description: UiO-67 is the BPDC-extended sibling of UiO-66, giving a larger pore (~12 Å) at the cost of slightly reduced thermal stability. The fcu topology is retained, and the framework tolerates water vapour better than most expanded MOFs.

references.authors: Cavka, J. H.; Jakobsen, S.; Olsbye, U.; Guillou, N.; Lamberti, C.; Bordiga, S.; Lillerud, K. P.
references.title: A New Zirconium Inorganic Building Brick Forming Metal Organic Frameworks with Exceptional Stability

name: UiO-68
description: UiO-68 completes the original isoreticular Zr-fcu trio (UiO-66/67/68) with the longest linker. Activation requires supercritical CO₂ to preserve the otherwise collapse-prone large pores.

references.authors: Cavka, J. H.; Jakobsen, S.; Olsbye, U.; Guillou, N.; Lamberti, C.; Bordiga, S.; Lillerud, K. P.
references.title: A New Zirconium Inorganic Building Brick Forming Metal Organic Frameworks with Exceptional Stability

name: PCN-222
description: PCN-222 (= MOF-545) combines 8-connected Zr6 nodes with the tetratopic TCPP porphyrin to give a mesoporous csq net with 37 Å hexagonal channels. Metal insertion (Fe, Mn, Co) into the porphyrin yields one of the most-studied MOF families for biomimetic catalysis and singlet-oxygen photochemistry.

references.authors: Feng, D.; Gu, Z.-Y.; Li, J.-R.; Jiang, H.-L.; Wei, Z.; Zhou, H.-C.
references.title: Zirconium-Metalloporphyrin PCN-222: Mesoporous Metal-Organic Frameworks with Ultrahigh Stability as Biomimetic Catalysts

name: PCN-224
description: PCN-224 reduces the Zr6 connectivity to 6 by combining it with the tetratopic TCPP, giving a cubic she net distinct from the hexagonal PCN-222. Its 3D mesoporous channels and accessible porphyrin pockets are widely used in CO₂ photoreduction and biomedical singlet-oxygen generation.

references.authors: Feng, D.; Chung, W.-C.; Wei, Z.; Gu, Z.-Y.; Jiang, H.-L.; Chen, Y.-P.; Darensbourg, D. J.; Zhou, H.-C.
references.title: Construction of Ultrastable Porphyrin Zr Metal-Organic Frameworks through Linker Elimination

name: PCN-250(Fe)
description: PCN-250 is built from the robust Fe3(μ3-O)(CO2)6 trimer with the tetratopic ABTC linker, giving the soc net. Its high water and acid stability place it among the most industrially-viable MOFs for flue-gas CO₂ capture; mixed-metal Fe/Co variants are commonly studied.

references.authors: Feng, D.; Wang, K.; Wei, Z.; Chen, Y.-P.; Simon, C. M.; Arvapally, R. K.; Martin, R. L.; Bosch, M.; Liu, T.-F.; Fordham, S.; Yuan, D.; Omary, M. A.; Haranczyk, M.; Smit, B.; Zhou, H.-C.
references.title: Kinetically tuned dimensional augmentation as a versatile synthetic route towards robust metal-organic frameworks

name: DUT-67(Zr)
description: DUT-67 pairs Zr6 nodes with the bent thiophene-2,5-dicarboxylate to give a reo-type net with rectangular and triangular channels. The reduced 8-connectivity yields defect-tolerant catalysis; the framework is unusually robust toward SO₂.

references.authors: Bon, V.; Senkovska, I.; Baburin, I. A.; Kaskel, S.
references.title: Zr-MOFs as Heterogeneous Catalysts: Modification with Sulfonic and Phosphonic Acid Groups

name: NU-1101
description: NU-1101 combines the 8-connected Zr6 node with the pyrene-cored PyTBP tetratopic linker. The mesoporous csq net achieves high methane deliverable capacity in the 5–65 bar range, useful for adsorbed natural gas tanks.

references.authors: Gomez-Gualdron, D. A.; Gutov, O. V.; Krungleviciute, V.; Borah, B.; Mondloch, J. E.; Hupp, J. T.; Yildirim, T.; Farha, O. K.; Snurr, R. Q.
references.title: Computational Design of Metal-Organic Frameworks Based on Stable Zirconium Building Units for Storage and Delivery of Methane

name: NU-1501-Al
description: NU-1501-Al holds a current high-water mark for BET surface area among Al-MOFs at 7,310 m²/g, with one of the largest deliverable capacities for gravimetric high-pressure H₂ and CH₄. Its trinuclear Al-trimer SBU and hexagonal acs net deliver durability matched to industrial pressures.

references.authors: Chen, Z.; Li, P.; Anderson, R.; Wang, X.; Zhang, X.; Robison, L.; Redfern, L. R.; Moribe, S.; Islamoglu, T.; Gómez-Gualdrón, D. A.; Yildirim, T.; Stoddart, J. F.; Farha, O. K.
references.title: Balancing volumetric and gravimetric uptake in highly porous materials for clean energy

name: MIL-53(Al)
description: MIL-53(Al) is the Al(III) analogue of MIL-53, sharing the diamond-shaped channels and breathing behaviour but with greater thermal and hydrothermal stability. Its narrow-pore ↔ large-pore transition is widely used as a model for guest-induced flexibility.

references.authors: Loiseau, T.; Serre, C.; Huguenard, C.; Fink, G.; Taulelle, F.; Henry, M.; Bataille, T.; Férey, G.
references.title: A Rationale for the Large Breathing of the Porous Aluminum Terephthalate (MIL-53) Upon Hydration

name: MIL-53(Fe)
description: MIL-53(Fe) is the iron isostructural analogue with reduced surface area but enhanced redox activity and biocompatibility. Its visible-light absorption from Fe³⁺ d–d transitions enables photocatalytic applications absent from the Al/Cr analogues.

references.authors: Whitfield, T. R.; Wang, X.; Liu, L.; Jacobson, A. J.
references.title: Metal-Organic Frameworks Based on Iron Oxide Octahedral Chains Connected by Benzenedicarboxylate Dianions

name: MIL-100(Fe)-variant
description: An alternative iron preparation of MIL-100 with similar mesoporous mtn structure but distinct activation behaviour. Largely overlapping with the canonical MIL-100(Fe) entry; included to anchor synthesis variants.

references.authors: Horcajada, P.; Surblé, S.; Serre, C.; Hong, D.-Y.; Seo, Y.-K.; Chang, J.-S.; Grenèche, J.-M.; Margiolaki, I.; Férey, G.
references.title: Synthesis and Catalytic Properties of MIL-100(Fe), an Iron(III) Carboxylate with Large Pores

name: MIL-100(Cr)
description: MIL-100(Cr) was the first hierarchical mesoporous chromium MOF, built from Cr3O trimers and BTC connectors arranged in two cage sizes (25 and 29 Å). The framework is exceptionally stable in water and acidic media, prized for heterogeneous catalysis.

references.authors: Férey, G.; Serre, C.; Mellot-Draznieks, C.; Millange, F.; Surblé, S.; Dutour, J.; Margiolaki, I.
references.title: A Hybrid Solid with Giant Pores Prepared by a Combination of Targeted Chemistry, Simulation, and Powder Diffraction

name: MIL-100(Al)
description: MIL-100(Al) extends the giant-pore Cr trimer family to non-toxic Al(III). Its water uptake step at intermediate humidity, combined with hydrothermal stability, makes it a leading candidate for sorption-driven cooling.

references.authors: Volkringer, C.; Popov, D.; Loiseau, T.; Férey, G.; Burghammer, M.; Riekel, C.; Haouas, M.; Taulelle, F.
references.title: Synthesis, Single-Crystal X-Ray Microdiffraction, and NMR Characterizations of the Giant Pore MOF Aluminum Trimesate MIL-100(Al)

name: MIL-101(Fe)
description: MIL-101(Fe) is the iron isostructural to MIL-101(Cr). Lower toxicity makes it the framework of choice for in vivo MOF-based drug delivery; visible-light-active Fe³⁺ centres enable photoredox catalysis.

references.authors: Horcajada, P.; Serre, C.; Vallet-Regí, M.; Sebban, M.; Taulelle, F.; Férey, G.
references.title: Metal-Organic Frameworks as Efficient Materials for Drug Delivery

name: MIL-88B(Fe)
description: MIL-88B(Fe) is a flexible Fe-trimer carboxylate with reversible solvent-driven breathing of ~100 % unit-cell volume change between dry and hydrated forms. The same acs topology underlies the larger MIL-88C/D members.

references.authors: Surblé, S.; Serre, C.; Mellot-Draznieks, C.; Millange, F.; Férey, G.
references.title: A New Isoreticular Class of Metal-Organic-Frameworks with the MIL-88 Topology

name: MIL-127(Fe)
description: MIL-127(Fe) extends the soc topology to bio-friendly iron centres. Robust to water and physiological media, it is among the most studied MOFs for nanoparticle drug-delivery formulations and for CO₂/H₂O competitive adsorption studies.

references.authors: Liu, Y.; Eubank, J. F.; Cairns, A. J.; Eckert, J.; Kravtsov, V. Ch.; Luebke, R.; Eddaoudi, M.
references.title: Assembly of Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs) Based on Indium-Trimer Building Blocks: A Porous MOF with soc Topology and High Hydrogen Storage

name: MIL-160(Al)
description: MIL-160 is an Al-MOF built from the biomass-derived furan-2,5-dicarboxylate, giving a step-shaped water isotherm centred near 8 % RH. Its hydrothermal cycling stability, low regeneration temperature, and green linker make it a flagship MOF for adsorption-driven heat pumps.

references.authors: Cadiau, A.; Lee, J. S.; Damasceno Borges, D.; Fabry, P.; Devic, T.; Wharmby, M. T.; Martineau, C.; Foucher, D.; Taulelle, F.; Jun, C.-H.; Hwang, Y. K.; Stock, N.; De Lange, M. F.; Kapteijn, F.; Gascon, J.; Maurin, G.; Chang, J.-S.; Serre, C.
references.title: Design of Hydrophilic Metal Organic Framework Water Adsorbents for Heat Reallocation

name: MIL-140A
description: MIL-140A is a denser Zr-BDC framework formed from infinite zirconium-oxide chains instead of the discrete Zr6 cluster of UiO-66. Its 1D channels and exceptional acid/base stability complement UiO-66 for separations under harsh conditions.

references.authors: Guillerm, V.; Ragon, F.; Dan-Hardi, M.; Devic, T.; Vishnuvarthan, M.; Campo, B.; Vimont, A.; Clet, G.; Yang, Q.; Maurin, G.; Férey, G.; Vittadini, A.; Gross, S.; Serre, C.
references.title: A Series of Isoreticular, Highly Stable, Porous Zirconium Oxide Based Metal-Organic Frameworks

name: CAU-1
description: CAU-1 (Christian-Albrechts-Universität-1) introduced the Al8 octanuclear cluster as an SBU. Its amine groups direct selective CO₂ adsorption and serve as docking sites for post-synthetic functionalization.

references.authors: Ahnfeldt, T.; Guillou, N.; Gunzelmann, D.; Margiolaki, I.; Loiseau, T.; Férey, G.; Senker, J.; Stock, N.
references.title: [Al4(OH)2(OCH3)4(NH2-BDC)3]·xH2O: A 12-Connected Porous Metal-Organic Framework with an Unprecedented Aluminum-Containing Brick

name: CAU-10-H
description: CAU-10-H displays a sharp S-shaped water isotherm at intermediate humidity (~18 % RH) and exceptional cycling stability — both criteria for sorption-driven heat pumps. The Al-IPA framework is among a small handful of MOFs to enter pilot-scale heat-transformation tests.

references.authors: Reinsch, H.; van der Veen, M. A.; Gil, B.; Marszalek, B.; Verbiest, T.; de Vos, D.; Stock, N.
references.title: Structural Diversity, Sorption and Magnetic Properties of a New Family of Functionalized Aluminum-Based MOFs

name: CAU-17
description: CAU-17 is among the most porous Bi-based MOFs. Its non-toxic main-group metal, visible-light absorption, and high capacity for I₂ vapour make it an emerging candidate for nuclear off-gas treatment.

references.authors: Köppen, M.; Beyer, O.; Wuttke, S.; Lüning, U.; Stock, N.
references.title: Synthesis, Functionalisation and Post-Synthetic Modification of Bismuth Metal-Organic Frameworks

name: ZIF-7
description: ZIF-7 displays a gate-opening adsorption mechanism: framework deformation gives a step in gas isotherms at characteristic pressures, enabling selective separations. Sodalite topology, same as ZIF-8 but with bulkier benzimidazolate.

references.authors: Park, K. S.; Ni, Z.; Côté, A. P.; Choi, J. Y.; Huang, R.; Uribe-Romo, F. J.; Chae, H. K.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Exceptional Chemical and Thermal Stability of Zeolitic Imidazolate Frameworks

name: ZIF-11
description: ZIF-11 is the rho-topology polymorph of Zn-benzimidazolate, with a larger cage than the sod-topology ZIF-7. The narrow aperture between cages drives kinetic separation of small gases.

references.authors: Park, K. S.; Ni, Z.; Côté, A. P.; Choi, J. Y.; Huang, R.; Uribe-Romo, F. J.; Chae, H. K.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Exceptional Chemical and Thermal Stability of Zeolitic Imidazolate Frameworks

name: ZIF-71
description: ZIF-71 carries chloro substituents on the imidazolate linker, increasing hydrophobicity. Its rho net with 4.8 Å apertures shows excellent selectivity for alcohols over water — the basis of MOF-based pervaporation membranes for bio-ethanol recovery.

references.authors: Banerjee, R.; Phan, A.; Wang, B.; Knobler, C.; Furukawa, H.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: High-Throughput Synthesis of Zeolitic Imidazolate Frameworks and Application to CO₂ Capture

name: ZIF-95
description: ZIF-95 exhibits one of the highest CO₂ uptakes per surface area among ZIFs at sub-ambient temperatures, owing to its narrow apertures and high CO₂-philic chloro groups in the cavity. The poz topology is unique to halogenated benzimidazolates.

references.authors: Wang, B.; Côté, A. P.; Furukawa, H.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Colossal cages in zeolitic imidazolate frameworks as selective carbon dioxide reservoirs

name: NOTT-101
description: NOTT-101 was a benchmark Cu-paddlewheel framework for H₂ and CH₄ storage at the time of its report, achieved through optimal Cu-BHB nbo topology. Activation requires careful supercritical CO₂ treatment to preserve porosity.

references.authors: Lin, X.; Telepeni, I.; Blake, A. J.; Dailly, A.; Brown, C. M.; Simmons, J. M.; Zoppi, M.; Walker, G. S.; Thomas, K. M.; Mays, T. J.; Hubberstey, P.; Champness, N. R.; Schröder, M.
references.title: High Capacity Hydrogen Adsorption in Cu(II) Tetracarboxylate Framework Materials

name: PCN-6
description: PCN-6 is the doubly interpenetrated polymorph of PCN-6'; interpenetration roughly doubles the volumetric H₂ uptake versus the open framework. A widely cited early example of designed catenation enhancing physisorption.

references.authors: Sun, D.; Ma, S.; Ke, Y.; Collins, D. J.; Zhou, H.-C.
references.title: An Interweaving MOF with High Hydrogen Uptake

name: NU-100
description: NU-100 (also PCN-610) held the record for total H₂ uptake among MOFs at the time of its publication, owing to its very high specific surface area and pore volume. Computationally designed prior to synthesis — an early triumph of in silico MOF discovery.

references.authors: Farha, O. K.; Yazaydın, A. Ö.; Eryazici, I.; Malliakas, C. D.; Hauser, B. G.; Kanatzidis, M. G.; Nguyen, S. T.; Snurr, R. Q.; Hupp, J. T.
references.title: De novo synthesis of a metal-organic framework material featuring ultrahigh surface area and gas storage capacities

name: NU-110
description: NU-110 held the BET surface-area world record (7,140 m²/g) for several years. Its ntt net is engineered by inserting additional phenyl–alkyne spacers into the NU-100 backbone; activation requires supercritical CO₂ to prevent collapse.

references.authors: Farha, O. K.; Eryazici, I.; Jeong, N. C.; Hauser, B. G.; Wilmer, C. E.; Sarjeant, A. A.; Snurr, R. Q.; Nguyen, S. T.; Yazaydın, A. Ö.; Hupp, J. T.
references.title: Metal-Organic Framework Materials with Ultrahigh Surface Areas: Is the Sky the Limit?

name: MOF-505
description: MOF-505 (also NOTT-100) introduced the Cu-paddlewheel + biphenyltetracarboxylate nbo platform that became the parent of an entire family of high-surface-area MOFs (NOTT-101 to -109).

references.authors: Chen, B.; Ockwig, N. W.; Millward, A. R.; Contreras, D. S.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: High H₂ Adsorption in a Microporous Metal-Organic Framework with Open Metal Sites

name: UMCM-1
description: UMCM-1 was the first reported mesoporous Zn-MOF combining two linker geometries (di- and tritopic). Its hexagonal microporous + mesoporous channel system delivers both very high gravimetric storage and accessible large molecules.

references.authors: Koh, K.; Wong-Foy, A. G.; Matzger, A. J.
references.title: A Crystalline Mesoporous Coordination Copolymer with High Microporosity

name: UMCM-2
description: UMCM-2 swaps BDC for the larger thieno[3,2-b]thiophene-2,5-dicarboxylate, achieving a 5,200 m²/g BET area. Among the highest reported surface areas for any Zn-MOF.

references.authors: Koh, K.; Wong-Foy, A. G.; Matzger, A. J.
references.title: A Porous Coordination Copolymer with over 5,000 m² g⁻¹ BET Surface Area

name: SIFSIX-3-Zn
description: SIFSIX-3-Zn is a primitive cubic framework with pyrazine in the equatorial plane and SiF6²⁻ as the axial pillar. The narrow 3.84 Å channels and CO₂-philic fluorine sites give it record-level CO₂ uptake at low partial pressures, valuable for direct-air-capture concepts.

references.authors: Nugent, P.; Belmabkhout, Y.; Burd, S. D.; Cairns, A. J.; Luebke, R.; Forrest, K.; Pham, T.; Ma, S.; Space, B.; Wojtas, L.; Eddaoudi, M.; Zaworotko, M. J.
references.title: Porous materials with optimal adsorption thermodynamics and kinetics for CO₂ separation

name: SIFSIX-3-Cu
description: SIFSIX-3-Cu shrinks the SIFSIX-3-Zn pore to 3.50 Å through smaller Cu(II)–N bonds. The result is an exceptional CO₂ uptake at very low partial pressures (ppm-level), making it a leading material for direct air capture.

references.authors: Shekhah, O.; Belmabkhout, Y.; Chen, Z.; Guillerm, V.; Cairns, A.; Adil, K.; Eddaoudi, M.
references.title: Made-to-order metal-organic frameworks for trace carbon dioxide removal and air capture

name: TIFSIX-2-Cu-i
description: TIFSIX-2-Cu-i is a doubly interpenetrated SIFSIX analogue with TiF6²⁻ pillars. The interpenetration creates strong binding pockets that distinguish acetylene from ethylene with record selectivity — a key step in olefin purification.

references.authors: Cui, X.; Chen, K.; Xing, H.; Yang, Q.; Krishna, R.; Bao, Z.; Wu, H.; Zhou, W.; Dong, X.; Han, Y.; Li, B.; Ren, Q.; Zaworotko, M. J.; Chen, B.
references.title: Pore chemistry and size control in hybrid porous materials for acetylene capture from ethylene

name: Mg₂(dobpdc)
description: Mg₂(dobpdc) is an expanded analogue of Mg-MOF-74 with the BPDC-style linker, giving 21 Å hexagonal channels lined by coordinatively unsaturated Mg²⁺ sites. Mainly studied as a scaffold for amine functionalisation (mmen, dmen, dmpn).

references.authors: McDonald, T. M.; Lee, W. R.; Mason, J. A.; Wiers, B. M.; Hong, C. S.; Long, J. R.
references.title: Capture of Carbon Dioxide from Air and Flue Gas in the Alkylamine-Appended Metal-Organic Framework mmen-Mg₂(dobpdc)

name: mmen-Mg₂(dobpdc)
description: Diamine-appended Mg₂(dobpdc) shows a cooperative, step-shaped CO₂ isotherm caused by chain-of-ammonium-carbamate formation along the open-metal sites. This is the prototype of a new class of CO₂ sorbents combining MOF stability with amine-like reactivity at low partial pressures.

references.authors: McDonald, T. M.; Mason, J. A.; Kong, X.; Bloch, E. D.; Gygi, D.; Dani, A.; Crocellà, V.; Giordanino, F.; Odoh, S. O.; Drisdell, W. S.; Vlaisavljevich, B.; Dzubak, A. L.; Poloni, R.; Schnell, S. K.; Planas, N.; Lee, K.; Pascal, T.; Wan, L. F.; Prendergast, D.; Neaton, J. B.; Smit, B.; Kortright, J. B.; Gagliardi, L.; Bordiga, S.; Reimer, J. A.; Long, J. R.
references.title: Cooperative insertion of CO₂ in diamine-appended metal-organic frameworks

name: DUT-49
description: DUT-49 famously exhibits negative gas adsorption — pressure-induced contraction releases methane from the framework, producing a step-wise decrease in uptake with increasing pressure. The carbazole-based tetracarboxylate linker buckles under guest pressure, opening a new design axis for stimuli-responsive MOFs.

references.authors: Krause, S.; Bon, V.; Senkovska, I.; Stoeck, U.; Wallacher, D.; Többens, D. M.; Zander, S.; Pillai, R. S.; Maurin, G.; Coudert, F.-X.; Kaskel, S.
references.title: A pressure-amplifying framework material with negative gas adsorption transitions

name: Co(BDP)
description: Co(BDP) undergoes a series of structural phase transitions from a closed to an open form as gas pressure increases, producing isotherms with multiple steps. The pyrazolate-based linker confers exceptional thermal and chemical stability.

references.authors: Choi, H. J.; Dincă, M.; Long, J. R.
references.title: Broadly Hysteretic H₂ Adsorption in the Microporous Metal-Organic Framework Co(1,4-benzenedipyrazolate)

name: DMOF-1
description: DMOF-1 is the canonical pillared paddlewheel framework: Zn₂ paddlewheels linked by BDC into 2D sheets, pillared by DABCO into a 3D pcu net. Its rectangular channels and tunable functionality (via BDC modification) make DMOF a workhorse platform for separations research.

references.authors: Dybtsev, D. N.; Chun, H.; Kim, K.
references.title: Rigid and Flexible: A Highly Porous Metal-Organic Framework with Unusual Guest-Dependent Dynamic Behavior

name: Cu₃(HHTP)₂
description: Cu₃(HHTP)₂ is a 2D conductive MOF built from Cu(II) bis(catecholate) nodes and triphenylene linkers stacked into hexagonal columns. The π-d conjugation gives in-plane conductivity around 0.2 S/cm — among the highest for a MOF — enabling electronic device applications.

references.authors: Hmadeh, M.; Lu, Z.; Liu, Z.; Gándara, F.; Furukawa, H.; Wan, S.; Augustyn, V.; Chang, R.; Liao, L.; Zhou, F.; Perre, E.; Ozolins, V.; Suenaga, K.; Duan, X.; Dunn, B.; Yamamto, Y.; Terasaki, O.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: New Porous Crystals of Extended Metal-Catecholates

name: Ni₃(HITP)₂
description: Ni₃(HITP)₂ is the bench-mark conductive MOF: a 2D hexagonal sheet of Ni(II) bis(diimine) units stacked into channels, with in-plane conductivity above 40 S/cm. The graphite-like π-stacking and porosity together support electrocatalysis, capacitive energy storage, and pollutant sensing.

references.authors: Sheberla, D.; Sun, L.; Blood-Forsythe, M. A.; Er, S.; Wade, C. R.; Brozek, C. K.; Aspuru-Guzik, A.; Dincă, M.
references.title: High Electrical Conductivity in Ni₃(2,3,6,7,10,11-hexaiminotriphenylene)₂, a Semiconducting Metal-Organic Graphene Analogue

name: MOF-76
description: MOF-76 is among the most studied luminescent MOFs. The Tb(III) ⁵D₄ → ⁷F₅ transition at 545 nm responds to guest exchange in the open coordination site, enabling ratiometric chemical sensing of anions and small molecules.

references.authors: Rosi, N. L.; Kim, J.; Eddaoudi, M.; Chen, B.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Rod Packings and Metal-Organic Frameworks Constructed from Rod-Shaped Secondary Building Units

name: Eu-BTC
description: Eu-BTC is the Eu(III) analogue of MOF-76, with characteristic red emission from the Eu(III) ⁵D₀ → ⁷F₂ transition near 615 nm. Tb/Eu mixtures form ratiometric luminescent thermometers used for nanoscale temperature mapping.

references.authors: Reineke, T. M.; Eddaoudi, M.; Fehr, M.; Kelley, D.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: From Condensed Lanthanide Coordination Solids to Microporous Frameworks Having Accessible Metal Sites

name: bio-MOF-1
description: bio-MOF-1 incorporates the nucleobase adenine as a structural component, giving an anionic framework that hosts cationic guests via ion exchange. Loaded with procainamide HCl, it demonstrated controlled drug release in physiological media, opening biocompatible MOF design.

references.authors: An, J.; Geib, S. J.; Rosi, N. L.
references.title: Cation-Triggered Drug Release from a Porous Zinc-Adeninate Metal-Organic Framework

name: MOF-2
description: MOF-2 introduced the Zn-paddlewheel + dicarboxylate layered platform that preceded MOF-5. Sheets of Zn₂(CO₂)₄ paddlewheels bridged by BDC form a sql net; weak inter-layer interactions limit permanent porosity, prompting the design of 3D MOF-5 the following year.

references.authors: Li, H.; Eddaoudi, M.; Groy, T. L.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Establishing Microporosity in Open Metal-Organic Frameworks: Gas Sorption Isotherms for Zn(BDC)·(DMF)(H2O)

name: MFM-300(In)
description: MFM-300(In) and its Al/Sc/Fe analogues are among the most chemically robust MOFs reported. The bridging hydroxyls hydrogen-bond strongly with acidic guests like SO₂ and H₂S, enabling unprecedented capture capacities at low partial pressures with full reversibility.

references.authors: Yang, S.; Sun, J.; Ramirez-Cuesta, A. J.; Callear, S. K.; David, W. I. F.; Anderson, D. P.; Newby, R.; Blake, A. J.; Parker, J. E.; Tang, C. C.; Schröder, M.
references.title: Selectivity and direct visualization of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide in a decorated porous host

name: MFM-300(Al)
description: MFM-300(Al) is the lighter and cheaper Al analogue of MFM-300(In), with even higher BET surface area. Detailed neutron diffraction has been used to visualise NH₃ binding at the hydroxyl groups — molecular-level insight rare among MOFs.

references.authors: Ibarra, I. A.; Yang, S.; Lin, X.; Blake, A. J.; Rizkallah, P. J.; Nowell, H.; Allan, D. R.; Champness, N. R.; Hubberstey, P.; Schröder, M.
references.title: Structures and H₂ adsorption properties of porous scandium metal-organic frameworks

name: MOF-841
description: MOF-841 was one of the top water-harvesting candidates identified in the Furukawa 2014 survey, with high uptake, sharp step at the relevant humidity, and reversibility across many cycles.

references.authors: Furukawa, H.; Gándara, F.; Zhang, Y.-B.; Jiang, J.; Queen, W. L.; Hudson, M. R.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Water Adsorption in Porous Metal–Organic Frameworks and Related Materials

name: NOTT-103
description: NOTT-103 incorporates an anthracene spacer in the tetracarboxylate, yielding extended channels and one of the higher H₂ uptakes in the NOTT family at 77 K.

references.authors: Lin, X.; Telepeni, I.; Blake, A. J.; Dailly, A.; Brown, C. M.; Simmons, J. M.; Zoppi, M.; Walker, G. S.; Thomas, K. M.; Mays, T. J.; Hubberstey, P.; Champness, N. R.; Schröder, M.
references.title: High Capacity Hydrogen Adsorption in Cu(II) Tetracarboxylate Framework Materials

name: PCN-12
description: PCN-12 was one of the early high-H₂-capacity MOFs, exploiting the methylene-bridged diisophthalate to give open Cu(II) sites in two distinct cage environments.

references.authors: Wang, X.-S.; Ma, S.; Sun, D.; Parkin, S.; Zhou, H.-C.
references.title: A Mesoporous Metal-Organic Framework with Permanent Porosity

name: NU-109

ligands.name: L
ligands.fullname: hexacarboxylate based on stiff aryl-alkyne core

description: NU-109 is the sister of NU-110 with a slightly shorter linker; together they pushed the BET surface-area record above 7,000 m²/g.

references.authors: Farha, O. K.; Eryazici, I.; Jeong, N. C.; Hauser, B. G.; Wilmer, C. E.; Sarjeant, A. A.; Snurr, R. Q.; Nguyen, S. T.; Yazaydın, A. Ö.; Hupp, J. T.
references.title: Metal-Organic Framework Materials with Ultrahigh Surface Areas: Is the Sky the Limit?

name: DUT-32
description: DUT-32 is the Kaskel-group ultrahigh-surface-area mixed-linker Zn4O framework, akin to UMCM-1 but with much larger BTCTB linkers, achieving 6,411 m²/g BET.

references.authors: Stoeck, U.; Krause, S.; Bon, V.; Senkovska, I.; Kaskel, S.
references.title: A highly porous metal-organic framework, constructed from a cuboctahedral super-molecular building block, with exceptionally high methane uptake

name: ZIF-69
description: ZIF-69 has the gme zeolite topology with nitro and chloro substituents lining the pores; the polar pore walls give one of the highest CO₂/N₂ selectivities among first-generation ZIFs.

references.authors: Banerjee, R.; Phan, A.; Wang, B.; Knobler, C.; Furukawa, H.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: High-Throughput Synthesis of Zeolitic Imidazolate Frameworks and Application to CO₂ Capture

name: ZIF-100
description: ZIF-100 features 35.6 Å cages — among the largest ever reported for a zeolitic imidazolate framework — accessed through narrow apertures that yield strong CO₂ selectivity.

references.authors: Wang, B.; Côté, A. P.; Furukawa, H.; O'Keeffe, M.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Colossal cages in zeolitic imidazolate frameworks as selective carbon dioxide reservoirs

name: Mn-MOF-74
description: Mn-MOF-74 features high-spin Mn(II) open metal sites that bind O₂ reversibly via partial electron transfer, enabling air separation. The same sites bind π-acidic guests with characteristic enthalpies useful for olefin purification.

references.authors: Zhou, W.; Wu, H.; Yildirim, T.
references.title: Enhanced H₂ Adsorption in Isostructural Metal-Organic Frameworks with Open Metal Sites: Strong Dependence of the Binding Strength on Metal Ions

name: UiO-66-Br
description: UiO-66-Br is a halogenated functional analogue of UiO-66, demonstrating the isoreticular substitution principle for Zr-MOFs. Bromine sites enable post-synthetic cross-coupling chemistry.

references.authors: Kandiah, M.; Nilsen, M. H.; Usseglio, S.; Jakobsen, S.; Olsbye, U.; Tilset, M.; Larabi, C.; Quadrelli, E. A.; Bonino, F.; Lillerud, K. P.
references.title: Synthesis and Stability of Tagged UiO-66 Zr-MOFs

name: MOF-180
description: MOF-180 is one of the early ultraporous Yaghi MOFs from the 2010 Science paper, combining the alkyne-extended BTE linker with Zn4O nodes into a hexagonal qom net.

references.authors: Furukawa, H.; Ko, N.; Go, Y. B.; Aratani, N.; Choi, S. B.; Choi, E.; Yazaydin, A. Ö.; Snurr, R. Q.; O'Keeffe, M.; Kim, J.; Yaghi, O. M.
references.title: Ultrahigh Porosity in Metal–Organic Frameworks

name: MIL-96(Al)
description: MIL-96(Al) has small ~4.5 Å windows and unusual hexagonal cavities lined by Al hydroxide trimers and corner-sharing AlO6 octahedra. The narrow apertures enable size-selective separations of small hydrocarbons.

references.authors: Loiseau, T.; Lecroq, L.; Volkringer, C.; Marrot, J.; Férey, G.; Haouas, M.; Taulelle, F.; Bourrelly, S.; Llewellyn, P. L.; Latroche, M.
references.title: MIL-96, a Porous Aluminum Trimesate 3D Structure Constructed from a Hexagonal Network of 18-Membered Rings and μ3-Oxo-Centered Trinuclear Units

name: ELM-11
description: ELM-11 is a textbook example of a soft (flexible) MOF: a non-porous closed phase opens cooperatively above a threshold gas pressure (the 'gate-opening' pressure), giving step-shaped isotherms exploited for kinetic separations.

references.authors: Onishi, S.; Ohmori, T.; Ohkubo, T.; Noguchi, H.; Di, L.; Hanzawa, Y.; Kanoh, H.; Kaneko, K.
references.title: Highly enhanced adsorption of CO₂ on flexible interpenetrated networks of Cu²⁺/BF₄⁻/4,4'-bpy

name: Cu₃(BHT)
description: Cu₃(BHT) is a 2D coordination polymer with metallic in-plane conductivity (~1500 S/cm in films) — among the highest reported for any coordination material. Single-layer flakes exhibit semiconductor-to-metal transitions and very-low-temperature superconductivity.

references.authors: Huang, X.; Sheng, P.; Tu, Z.; Zhang, F.; Wang, J.; Geng, H.; Zou, Y.; Di, C.-A.; Yi, Y.; Sun, Y.; Xu, W.; Zhu, D.
references.title: A two-dimensional π-d conjugated coordination polymer with extremely high electrical conductivity and ambipolar transport behaviour

name: SIFSIX-2-Cu
description: SIFSIX-2-Cu is the open (non-interpenetrated) version of the SIFSIX family with extended dipyridylacetylene linkers, giving 13 Å square channels.

references.authors: Nugent, P.; Belmabkhout, Y.; Burd, S. D.; Cairns, A. J.; Luebke, R.; Forrest, K.; Pham, T.; Ma, S.; Space, B.; Wojtas, L.; Eddaoudi, M.; Zaworotko, M. J.
references.title: Porous materials with optimal adsorption thermodynamics and kinetics for CO₂ separation

name: Ni-CPO-27
description: Ni-CPO-27 (the SINTEF naming for Ni-MOF-74) is the high-thermal-stability open-metal-site framework most widely used for catalysis and CO₂ chemistry; included as a name-and-CIF cross-reference for Ni-MOF-74.

references.authors: Dietzel, P. D. C.; Panella, B.; Hirscher, M.; Blom, R.; Fjellvåg, H.
references.title: Hydrogen adsorption in a nickel based coordination polymer with open metal sites in the cylindrical cavities of the desolvated framework

name: FMOF-1
description: FMOF-1 is a superhydrophobic Ag-triazolate MOF lined entirely with –CF₃ groups. It excludes water vapour even at 100 % RH while readily adsorbing hydrocarbons, making it a model material for fuel/water separation.

references.authors: Yang, C.; Wang, X.; Omary, M. A.
references.title: Fluorous Metal-Organic Frameworks for High-Density Gas Adsorption

name: DUT-9
description: DUT-9 is a mesoporous Ni-BTB MOF based on the rare pentanuclear Ni5O2 cluster, demonstrating expansion of MOF topology by introducing high-nuclearity transition-metal SBUs.

references.authors: Gedrich, K.; Senkovska, I.; Klein, N.; Stoeck, U.; Henschel, A.; Lohe, M. R.; Baburin, I. A.; Mueller, U.; Kaskel, S.
references.title: A Highly Porous Metal-Organic Framework with Open Nickel Sites

name: SIM-1
description: SIM-1 (Substituted Imidazolate Material-1) is an isoreticular ZIF-8 analogue with a methyl-aldehyde imidazolate. The polar aldehyde lining enhances CO₂ affinity and provides handles for post-synthetic functionalisation; it has been deployed as gas-separation membrane films.

references.authors: Aguado, S.; Canivet, J.; Farrusseng, D.
references.title: Engineering structured MOF at nano and macroscales for catalysis and separation

name: BUT-22
description: BUT-22 belongs to a series of Beijing-University-of-Technology Zr-MOFs constructed for high water and acid stability, fitting the Zr6 + extended dicarboxylate fcu pattern.

references.authors: Wang, B.; Lv, X.-L.; Feng, D.; Xie, L.-H.; Zhang, J.; Li, M.; Xie, Y.; Li, J.-R.; Zhou, H.-C.
references.title: Highly Stable Zr(IV)-Based Metal-Organic Frameworks for the Detection and Removal of Antibiotics and Organic Explosives in Water

name: HKUST-1-Ru
description: Ru-HKUST-1 is the diruthenium analogue of the famous Cu-HKUST-1 paddlewheel framework. Mixed-valent Ru₂⁵⁺ units provide accessible redox states for organic catalysis, distinguishing it functionally from the Cu(II) parent.

references.authors: Kozachuk, O.; Yusenko, K.; Noei, H.; Wang, Y.; Walleck, S.; Glaser, T.; Fischer, R. A.
references.title: Solvothermal Growth of a Ruthenium MOF Featuring HKUST-1 Structure on Functionalized Surfaces

name: In-soc-MOF
description: In-soc-MOF is the prototype soc (square-octahedron-cubic) net combining a trinuclear In(III) trimer with a planar 4-connecting tetracarboxylate linker. The platform inspired robust analogues such as PCN-250 (Fe) and DUT-67-class variants.

references.authors: Liu, Y.; Eubank, J. F.; Cairns, A. J.; Eckert, J.; Kravtsov, V. Ch.; Luebke, R.; Eddaoudi, M.
references.title: Assembly of Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs) Based on Indium-Trimer Building Blocks: A Porous MOF with soc Topology and High Hydrogen Storage

name: NU-1500-Fe
description: NU-1500-Fe is the iron analogue of the NU-1500/NU-1501 Al family. The triangular acs topology with trinuclear trimers offers high deliverable hydrogen capacity at moderate pressures.

references.authors: Chen, Z.; Li, P.; Anderson, R.; Wang, X.; Zhang, X.; Robison, L.; Redfern, L. R.; Moribe, S.; Islamoglu, T.; Gómez-Gualdrón, D. A.; Yildirim, T.; Stoddart, J. F.; Farha, O. K.
references.title: Balancing volumetric and gravimetric uptake in highly porous materials for clean energy

name: PCN-333(Al)
description: PCN-333(Al) features 55 Å mesoporous cages — large enough to encapsulate whole enzymes such as horseradish peroxidase, cytochrome c, and microperoxidase-11. Encapsulation preserves activity while gaining MOF robustness.

references.authors: Feng, D.; Liu, T.-F.; Su, J.; Bosch, M.; Wei, Z.; Wan, W.; Yuan, D.; Chen, Y.-P.; Wang, X.; Wang, K.; Lian, X.; Gu, Z.-Y.; Park, J.; Zou, X.; Zhou, H.-C.
references.title: Stable metal-organic frameworks containing single-molecule traps for enzyme encapsulation
